channel.title: Comments for page "Secure Facilities Locations"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Secure Facilities Locations"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have written some tales (as far as I can tell, the first) set at Armed Bio-Containment Area-14. Would it be appropriate to link them here? I already added all the other SCPs I could find that are contained there to the list.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - MAST (Collab Pages) - Open
item.content:encoded: Thanks stormbreath!

item.title: Staff Post - MAST (Collab Pages) - Open
item.content:encoded: After some discussion between MAST, we've decided this page will no longer be locked and is now an open collaboration. You can go ahead and edit the entry yourself, and add new entries in the future.

item.title: Also Request
item.content:encoded: On the same note as Grigori's request above: could someone please edit the Site-43 entry to link to my Site dossier, and also add SCP-5382, SCP-5494 and SCP-5520 to the list? I won't add more later, but those three are constituent of the setting.

item.title: 2020 MAST Edit
item.content:encoded: The tag on this page has been changed from guide to collaboration and the page has been unlocked. It is now under the remit of the collaboration log pruning subteam of MAST and all site members are invited to add their own secure facilities to the guide as long as they meet the requirements of at least 5 articles by 3 different authors. Improper additions will be regularly pruned by staff.

item.title: Request
item.content:encoded:
Could someone with privileges on this page change "Site-91" to contain the following link:1 http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/hecatoncheires-cycle-hub

Moreover, could you add: SCP-5612, SCP-3743 and SCP-5079 to the "Objects contained" bit at the end of the entry on this page.

Thanks in advance!

Footnotes
1. which is a Hub but also a Site Dossier

item.title: Staff Post - MAST Moderator
item.content:encoded: Site-56 has been added to the page.

item.title: Requesting addition of Site-56
item.content:encoded:
Noting that I've written several articles centered around Site-56, which is arguably one of the central backdrops of The Trashfire.

Site-56

Established in the Black Rock Desert of Nevada, May 1972, Site-56 was intended to serve as the base of operations for the enactment of the Kraken Protocol within the American Southwest. Over time, Site-56 took on increasing responsibilities, eventually transitioning into a full-fledged containment and administration site; however, recent Overwatch audits suggest its expansion has surpassed its budgetary and logistical limitations, and personnel frequently cite its "labyrinthine" structure as a detriment to day-to-day operations. Objects contained on-site include SCP-4495 and SCP-5444, with select offsite anomalies such as SCP-5994 falling under its jurisdiction as well.

item.title: Re: MAST - Guides Subteam
item.content:encoded: Thanks Malyce!

item.title: MAST - Guides Subteam
item.content:encoded: This will be added shortly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, Zyn!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting that I've poked the MAST/maintenance staff about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've used Site-43 for three SCPs and two very long tales, the Site and its personnel being core elements of each piece. (I'm also working on three more SCPs, one more tale and a Site dossier/stealth alternate history SCP story all featuring the Site.)

Plus, as mentioned upthread, there's no sites in Canada yet.

I'd be thrilled to get an entry here, à la:

Site-43 (Research and Containment Site-43)

Site-43 is situated on the southeastern shore of Lake Huron, one kilometre below sea level in Lambton County, Ontario, Canada. It comprises containment wings for low- to medium-risk anomalies, research divisions arranged in a pseudoacademic structure, and abatement refineries for esoteric substances. Objects contained at this site include: SCP-5056, SCP-5109, and SCP-5243.

Not trying to dogpile or anything! Just timed to look that way.

item.title: Re: Request for Site-91 to be added
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: Re: Request for Site-91 to be added
item.content:encoded: Approved and page updated

item.title: Re: Requesting Addition of Site-87
item.content:encoded: Approved and page Updated

item.title: Requesting Addition of Site-87
item.content:encoded:
Riding on Grigori's coattails here, slightly, didn't realize that you could request additions.

I don't know why I haven't done this yet. S & C Plastics is almost eight years old.

Site-87

Site-87 is a research site, established in September 1976 to oversee containment of a minor anomaly in the town of Sloth's Pit, Wisconsin, USA (pop. 17,291 as of 2020). In that same month, researchers discovered that Sloth's Pit was a highly active Nexus, an inhabited zone that has a high concentration of anomalous phenomena. Site-87's civilian front is S & C Plastics, and it possesses its own Task Force— Sigma-10, "The Sloth's Arm", dedicated to containment of anomalies within city limits. Citizens of Sloth's Pit are considered E-Class Personnel, and have knowledge of the Foundation insofar as 'a research organization is monitoring the town'. The primary goal of Site-87 is to research and catalog anomalies in Sloth's Pit, such as SCP-4040; however, it is also the headquarters of the Department of Multi-Universal Affairs, Nexological Studies, and serves as regional command for the Midwestern United States. A catalog of incidents that have taken place at Site-87 and within the Sloth's Pit Nexus zone can be found here.

item.title: Request for Site-91 to be added
item.content:encoded:
So I have been using Site-91 (which I made up, I think) as a backdrop and center of the three articles I've written so far. I was hoping we could get Site-91 added to this list:

Site-91

Established as Provisional Site-91 in 1986 shortly after the discovery of SCP-4612, the site was fully commissioned by Overwatch command the following year. Site-91 was originally adapted from its previous use to contain SCP-4612 (which was found in the substructure below the manor), but was expanded to a full containment facility, research lab and library. The site is located upon the grounds of a manor commonly known as Eckhart House; located in Yorkshire, England. The site's research focus is the containment and analysis of xenobiological specimens, with a secondary focus of the development of thaumaturgical countermeasures and containment of thaumaturgical artifacts. Serves as the primary station for MTF-Beta-777 (“Hecate’s Spear”) due to their familiarity and expertise in thaumaturgical analysis. Objects contained at this facility include: SCP-4612, SCP-4712, and SCP-5512.

item.title: Re: Request for Site-11 to be linked.
item.content:encoded: Page updated!

item.title: Request for Site-11 to be linked.
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I wrote and posted a full Site Dossier page for Site-11.
Link: http://www.scp-wiki.net/secure-facility-dossier-site-11

I was wondering if this link could be added to the Site-11 tab?

Thank you for your time.

item.title: Where are the iconic SCPs contained
item.content:encoded: I am just wondering

item.title: Re: Grammar error
item.content:encoded: Surprising that went unnoticed for so long. Thanks for bringing the error to staff attention! The correction has been made.

item.title: Grammar error
item.content:encoded:
Foundation facilities designated as Areas are clandestine facilities, meaning that civilians are not aware of its existence at all

“Its” in this context would refer to one subject, where there are multiple. Using “their” would be grammatically, contextually, and canonically correct.

item.title: Re: Requesting addition
item.content:encoded: Noting that I've received a message about the above entry. It looks like this Site meets/exceeds the criteria for a mention on the page. Staff can check me on this if it's incorrect.

item.title: Requesting addition
item.content:encoded:
Site-98 appears in and is referenced multiple times with 11 articles in total (2 of those articles are mine): SCP-179, SCP-1152, SCP-1287, SCP-2009, SCP-2115, SCP-2703, SCP-2716, SCP-4366, SCP-4797, SCP-4880, and even a 001 Proposal

If possible, I also have a brief description of the site if it is implemented.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I learned a bit more about data scraping and started putting together a list. (I'm still a fair chunk of work away from it being clean and finished.)

The big problem is, it's so huge a wall of text that I can't imagine it being useful to anyone. There are ways I could filter it down, or just focus on finding new Sites, or put it in a Comprehensive page (although I can't imagine that being super useful either). Of course, I'm not staff at all or involved in these decisions, I''m just volunteer datamining for the hell of it. Edit: Also, anyone on the tech team would probably be about 100 times better at this than me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe something to discuss with the technical team when there's some more groundwork laid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something like what Crayne did with MTFs originally could be used to match and update the existing Sites. My hodgepodge methods won't cut it for this.

For identifying new sites… that's more labor-intensive, but I can think of a way to somewhat automate it if there's a solution to the above.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, this page is pretty drastically out of date.

Agreed. Is there any feasible way of updating it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, this page is pretty drastically out of date. I started pulling data from a goolge search, but the average number of articles per Site listed is 55. (I was copying these over by hand and gave up due to inevitable typos.) That doesn't even count the newer Sites that must be missing.

I guess you could include links for each in collapsibles.

item.title: What did Bright do?
item.content:encoded: What happened to Site-12?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: About the distinction between "Site" and "Area"… Is it correct to say that "known to the public" in the former's case simply means that the Foundation doesn't go out of its way to conceal the existence of the facility if there's really no easy way of avoiding putting it where non-Foundation people would easily see it, and instead only focuses on concealing the nature of the facility's true purpose and internal activities/contents? Because I've seen more than a few SCP articles and tales where the term "Site" is applied to facilities that are obviously/necessarily being kept secret from the public, including "canon" facilities like Site-01 (e.g. in DJKaktus' third SCP-001 proposal).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This only mentions two SCP articles. For "one of the Foundation's highest-security containment facilities", it seems rather bland.

I personally don't feel like this entry is ready for the list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Requesting addition (Site-97):

[[include component:site name=Site-97|number=97|c0=#630|subtitle=[!-- --]]]
Site-97 was originally constructed around [[[SCP-1393]]], and since then has rapidly grown into one of the Foundation's highest-security containment facilities. Objects contained at this site include [[[SCP-4224]]], [[[SCP-1393]]]

Re-formatted. ~Zyn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
P.S: Please link Area-179 to my dossier for it:

No. That's not how this works. Something has to actually be able to survive on the mainsite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I already wrote a dossier for Area-179. Also, there's an error on the page that leaves out 2317 on the list of anomalies on 179.

Thanks.

P.S: Please link Area-179 to my dossier for it: http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/secure-facility-dossier-area-179

item.title: No Site-87?
item.content:encoded: I was just thinking that it's a bit surprising that there isn't an entry for Site-87 since the entire S & C Plastics Hub is mostly centered around it and has several tales by various authors set inside it.

item.title: Re: Requesting addition!
item.content:encoded: Zyn, you're an angel <3

item.title: Re: Requesting addition!
item.content:encoded: Thank you, stormbreath! I've updated the page with the Site-54 entry.

item.title: Re: Requesting addition!
item.content:encoded:
This is what you'd want to put in the article:

 [[include component:site name=Site-54|number=54|c0=#630|subtitle=(Manned Containment Site-54)]] Site-54 is a containment facility located in, around, and beneath the city of Leipzig, Germany. Specialising in the containment of those anomalies deemed 'partially uncontainable', the Site's staff are heavily armed, highly trained, and permanently on edge. Featuring an extensive rapid-response vehicle hangar (with transport both normal and anomalous) and wide array of holding cells, Site-54 is generally considered to be the Foundation site both best-prepared for the worst to happen, and the location at which said worst is most likely to occur. Objects currently under the facility's jurisdiction include: [[[SCP-265]]], [[[SCP-1911]]], [[[SCP-2668]]], [[[SCP-2790]]], [[[SCP-2856]]], [[[SCP-3663]]], and [[[SCP-4856]]].

There doesn't appear to be any actual rhyme or reason behind the colors of the label, unlike the equivalent Mobile Task Force Page (which is not the current one). The code above uses the most common default, the other colors on the page are grey (c0=#333 / Site-64), blue (c0=#006 / Site-76), purple (c0=#606 / Site-62) and green (c0=#030 / Site-01).

item.title: Re: Requesting addition!
item.content:encoded: I'm not certain what the typical protocol is for adding new entries, but this seems to have plenty of related articles so I think it's ready to be added. Can we get the source coding for the addition? Maybe have the little box label too?

item.title: Requesting addition!
item.content:encoded:
Site-54 (Manned Containment Site-54)

Site-54 is a containment facility located in, around, and beneath the city of Leipzig, Germany. Specialising in the containment of those anomalies deemed 'partially uncontainable', the Site's staff are heavily armed, highly trained, and permanently on edge. Featuring an extensive rapid-response vehicle hangar (with transport both normal and anomalous) and wide array of holding cells, Site-54 is generally considered to be the Foundation site both best-prepared for the worst to happen, and the location at which said worst is most likely to occur. Objects currently under the facility's jurisdiction include: SCP-265, SCP-1911, SCP-2668, SCP-2790, SCP-2856, SCP-3663, and SCP-4856.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe the colours in the numbered icons correspond with the 'Prefixes' listed near the top; Site-01, for example, would be Protected. I'm not sure offhand what the others are, one might be able to tell by the descriptions which categories fit.

E: Having looked at it more, I think:

  • Green (like Site-01) = Protected
  • Brown = Containment
  • Gray = Storage

… and maybe I'm not good at telling apart different shades of purple, or there's some Sites and Areas that don't necessarily fit the pattern (probably both). So maybe there's not really a system for it, and the colours just make the entries look nice.

item.title: Re: Site-19
item.content:encoded: Why isn't it necessary?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What do colors meaning?

item.title: Re: Site-19
item.content:encoded: Because it's not needed

item.title: Re: Site-19
item.content:encoded: Because no one’s written it yet.

item.title: Site-19
item.content:encoded: Why doesn't Site-19 have a dossier? It's the largest Foundation facility!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
perhaps it could be possible to make a list similar to http://www.scp-wiki.net/task-forces-complete-list#
This way it would be infinitely more easy to find info pertaining to a specific site. It would take a long time, but it would sure be worth it for writers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you Zyn.Your reply may help us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think there are any hard-and-fast rules, but generally the people who create Site Dossier pages are authors who have used those sites in their own articles. Since they essentially introduced those sites to the Foundation Universe, they would know best the vision behind the Site's in-universe and narrative creation.

item.title: Re: Canadian Site?
item.content:encoded: Wait！Para

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what are the criteria for creating a site page?

item.title: Re: Canadian Site?
item.content:encoded: thx

item.title: Re: Canadian Site?
item.content:encoded:
In looking through this guide, did that include the tab labelled 'Writing Guide'? Because it says right on this very page:

The entries here are intended as inspiration and a sample of how Foundation facilities might look like and is not intended to be all-inclusive or exhaustive. … If you are writing and want to use a facility, you are free to make up your own.

If you'd like to ask about others' ideas on the matter, or for people to suggest articles which take place in Canada, try creating a thread in, like, the Foundation Universe forum.

item.title: Canadian Site?
item.content:encoded:
I looked through the guide and found that there was not a single Site or Area that is located in Canada.

Is there a possible that there’re other authors want to help build up a Canadian Site as I would propose?

Or,if there’re any already existing Canadian sites/areas in any artical,pls let me konw. Thx a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay I'll work on that then get back to you with the SCPs. Might take a while though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AFAIK, Sites/areas are not added to the list unless they're featured in multiple articles. If you want to invent a Site, the way to do it is to write an article about what happens there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would it be possible for me to suggest an idea for an Area?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I rescind my previous question RE: criteria for adding a site, because Site-11 is literally Site-87 with a different number, so it's redundant. I'm a bit peeved, since Site-11 seems to be in all of two SCPs and all of one tale — none of which give the details of Site-11 that's presented here — and it kind of feels like whoever wrote that entry essentially took S & C Plastics and filed the numbers off. It probably wasn't the intent, but it leaves a foul taste in my mouth.
